```diff
+ 
+ ### EPIC: WAVEX_SAMPLE_PLAYBACK_EPIC
+ **Status**: IN_PROGRESS
+ **Priority**: Critical
+ **Started**: 2025-08-20
+ **Target Completion**: 2025-09-30
+ 
+ #### Goal
+ Implement comprehensive sample playback functionality on the Daisy Seed backend, including SD card sample loading, audio playback engine, and integration with the existing audio system.
+ 
+ #### Success Criteria
+ - SD card integration via SPI1 for sample storage and loading
+ - WAV file parsing and playback engine operational
+ - High-quality audio output with minimal latency
+ - Integration with existing UART inter-MCU communication
+ - Support for multiple sample formats and playback modes
+ - Stable audio performance without dropouts or glitches
+ 
+ #### Dependencies & Blockers
+ - Daisy Seed backend firmware operational (from WAVEX_DAISY_BACKEND_FIRMWARE_EPIC)
+ - UART inter-MCU communication stable (from WAVEX_UART_INTER_MCU_EPIC)
+ - Hardware connections for SD card and audio output verified
+ - libDaisy and DaisySP libraries with SD card and audio support
+ 
+ #### PHASE 1: SD CARD INTEGRATION — 🔄 IN_PROGRESS (0%)
+ **Goal**: Establish reliable SD card communication via SPI1 for sample storage and loading.
+ 
+ **Steps:**
+ 1. SD Card Hardware Setup
+    - Verify SPI1 pin connections (CS, SCLK, MOSI, MISO)
+    - Test SD card detection and initialization
+    - Acceptance: SD card mounts successfully and shows available storage
+ 2. SD Card Driver Implementation
+    - Implement FAT32 filesystem access using libDaisy
+    - Add file listing and navigation capabilities
+    - Implement sample file discovery (.wav, .aiff, etc.)
+    - Acceptance: Can list and access sample files on SD card
+ 3. Sample File Validation
+    - Implement WAV file header parsing and validation
+    - Check sample rate, bit depth, and channel compatibility
+    - Add error handling for corrupted or unsupported files
+    - Acceptance: WAV files properly parsed and validated
+ 
+ **Acceptance Tests:**
+ - SD card detected and mounted successfully
+ - Sample files listed and accessible
+ - WAV file headers properly parsed and validated
+ - No crashes or errors during file operations
+ 
+ #### PHASE 2: AUDIO PLAYBACK ENGINE — ⏳ PLANNED (0%)
+ **Goal**: Implement high-quality audio playback engine with support for various sample formats.
+ 
+ **Steps:**
+ 1. Sample Buffer Management
+    - Implement efficient sample buffering system
+    - Add support for different sample rates and bit depths
+    - Implement sample rate conversion if needed
+    - Acceptance: Samples load into memory without corruption
+ 2. Playback Engine Core
+    - Implement sample playback with proper timing
+    - Add pitch control and playback speed adjustment
+    - Implement loop points and one-shot playback modes
+    - Acceptance: Clean audio playback with no dropouts
+ 3. Audio Quality Optimization
+    - Implement interpolation for smooth playback
+    - Add anti-aliasing filters for pitch shifting
+    - Optimize buffer management for minimal latency
+    - Acceptance: High-quality audio output meets professional standards
+ 
+ **Acceptance Tests:**
+ - Sample playback engine operational with clean audio
+ - Pitch control and speed adjustment working smoothly
+ - Loop points and playback modes functioning correctly
+ - Audio quality suitable for professional audio applications
+ 
+ #### PHASE 3: SYSTEM INTEGRATION — ⏳ PLANNED (0%)
+ **Goal**: Integrate sample playback with existing UART communication and audio system.
+ 
+ **Steps:**
+ 1. UART Protocol Integration
+    - Add sample control messages to UART protocol
+    - Implement sample loading, playback control, and status reporting
+    - Add sample metadata transmission for frontend display
+    - Acceptance: Frontend can control sample playback via UART
+ 2. Audio System Integration
+    - Integrate sample playback with existing audio callback
+    - Ensure proper mixing with other audio sources
+    - Maintain stable audio timing and performance
+    - Acceptance: Sample playback integrates seamlessly with audio system
+ 3. Performance Optimization
+    - Optimize memory usage and buffer management
+    - Ensure stable performance under various load conditions
+    - Add performance monitoring and diagnostics
+    - Acceptance: System maintains stable performance during sample playback
+ 
+ **Acceptance Tests:**
+ - Sample playback fully integrated with UART communication
+ - Audio system stable during sample playback
+ - Performance monitoring shows optimal resource usage
+ - No interference with existing audio functionality
+ 
+ #### PHASE 4: TESTING AND VALIDATION — ⏳ PLANNED (0%)
+ **Goal**: Comprehensive testing and validation of the complete sample playback system.
+ 
+ **Steps:**
+ 1. Functionality Testing
+    - Test all sample formats and playback modes
+    - Verify SD card performance under various conditions
+    - Test error handling and recovery scenarios
+    - Acceptance: All functionality working reliably
+ 2. Performance Testing
+    - Measure audio latency and quality metrics
+    - Test with various sample rates and file sizes
+    - Verify stability during extended playback sessions
+    - Acceptance: Performance meets or exceeds requirements
+ 3. Integration Testing
+    - Test complete system with ESP32 frontend
+    - Verify UART communication stability during playback
+    - Test error conditions and recovery mechanisms
+    - Acceptance: Full system integration successful
+ 
+ **Acceptance Tests:**
+ - Complete sample playback functionality working reliably
+ - Performance metrics meet professional audio standards
+ - Full system integration successful with ESP32 frontend
+ - Stable operation under various load conditions
+ 
+ **Notes**
+ - Reference: `firmware/daisy/README.md` for current implementation status
+ - Architecture impact: Adds SD card SPI1 interface and sample playback engine to Daisy backend
+ - Critical for enabling professional sample-based audio functionality
  
   
```

```diff
  # workflow_state.md
- _Last updated: 2025-08-13_
+ _Last updated: 2025-08-20_
  
  ## State
  Phase: BLUEPRINT
  Status: NEEDS_PLAN_APPROVAL
- Epic Reference: WAVEX_UART_INTER_MCU_EPIC - PHASE 3: FIRMWARE IMPLEMENTATION (Daisy UART Implementation); WAVEX_DAISY_BACKEND_FIRMWARE_EPIC - PHASE 1: PROTOTYPE
+ Epic Reference: WAVEX_SAMPLE_PLAYBACK_EPIC - PHASE 1: SD CARD INTEGRATION
  
  ## Plan
- Blueprint additions for current goal, aligned with `docs/uart-inter-mcu-design.md` and architecture.
+ Blueprint for implementing sample playback functionality on Daisy Seed backend, focusing on SD card integration and audio playback engine.
+ 
+ SD Card Integration + Sample Playback Engine (Daisy) — Detailed Plan
  
- UART ISR Ring Buffer + Main Loop Restoration (Daisy) — Detailed Plan
  - Scope & Rationale
-   - Keep ISR minimal: byte capture only into a lock-free ring buffer to prevent overrun and avoid long ISRs that could affect audio.
-   - Parsing and all heavy work remain in the main loop.
- 
- - Data Structures (in `firmware/daisy/src/main.cpp`)
-   - Increase RX ring size to 512 bytes; keep `rx_head`, `rx_tail`, `rx_total`, and error counters as `volatile`.
-   - Add helpers:
-     - `static inline void rx_enqueue_from_isr(uint8_t ch)` with overflow handling (drop oldest, increment `g_uart_rx_errors`).
-     - `static int rx_dequeue_byte(uint8_t &out)` for main loop consumption.
- 
- - ISR Implementation (USART1 RX)
-   - Enable RXNE interrupt after UART init:
-     - `__HAL_UART_ENABLE_IT(&huart1, UART_IT_RXNE);` (H7 with FIFO disabled).
-   - Implement `extern "C" void USART1_IRQHandler(void)` that:
-     - Reads `ISR` flags; if RXNE set, read `RDR` and `rx_enqueue_from_isr(ch)`.
-     - Handles and clears error flags (ORE/FE/NE) by reading `RDR` and writing `ICR`.
-     - Avoids HAL re-arming one-byte IT paths to keep ISR short and deterministic.
-   - Keep NVIC priority at 5 as currently configured.
- 
- - Parser in Main Loop
-   - Add `PollAndParseUART()` that drains the ring buffer and assembles frames per protocol `[0xAA][len_hi][len_lo][type][payload][crc16]` using a small state machine.
-   - On complete, call `ProcessUARTMessage(frame, frame_len)` (reusing existing `ProtocolHandler`).
-   - Recover from desync by scanning for next `SYNC` if CRC/length invalid.
- 
- - TX Strategy (keep simple for now)
-   - Continue building response packets with `PrepareResponsePacket()` and send via blocking `HAL_UART_Transmit` in main loop when `MSG_DATA_REQUEST` arrives or when explicitly pushing heartbeat.
-   - Add optional 1 Hz heartbeat (`MSG_SYNC`) if link idle > 1s.
- 
- - Restore Main Loop Intent
-   - Remove temporary tight debug prints/spin delay.
-   - Start audio: `hw.StartAudio(AudioCallback);` and keep `Timebase::Tick1kHz` in callback.
-   - In `while(1)`: call `PollAndParseUART()`, service TX queue (send if `g_has_pending_data` and peer policy allows), and perform light status logging at low cadence.
- 
- - Testing Plan
-   - Stage A: ISR enabled, audio stopped → flood RX from ESP32; confirm no hangs, `rx_total` increases, no overrun.
-   - Stage B: Start audio; repeat flood; check audio stability and ISR timings.
-   - Stage C: End-to-end with ESP32: PARAM updates, SAMPLE_CTRL, PREVIEW_REQ; verify meters and wave chunks stream back; CRC passes.
-   - Log counters: RX/TX counts, error counts, overrun events.
- 
- - Docs/Rules updates
-   - Update `/.cursor/rules/epics.mdc` (Phase 3: Daisy UART Implementation progress) after implementation.
-   - If ISR-specific design nuances arise, add a short note to `docs/uart-inter-mcu-design.md` under Implementation Architecture (Daisy side).
- 
- Non-Goals (for this iteration)
- - No DMA-based RX/TX yet; evaluate after stable ISR ring buffer.
- - No hardware RTS/CTS; revisit only if overruns observed at higher baud rates.
+   - Implement comprehensive sample playback system using SD card storage via SPI1
+   - Enable professional-quality audio playback with minimal latency
+   - Integrate with existing UART communication and audio system
+   - Focus on WAV file support initially, expand to other formats later
+ 
+ - Hardware Integration (SPI1 SD Card)
+   - Configure SPI1 for SD card communication (CS, SCLK, MOSI, MISO)
+   - Implement SD card detection and initialization sequence
+   - Add FAT32 filesystem support using libDaisy SD card drivers
+   - Acceptance: SD card mounts successfully and shows available storage
+ 
+ - Sample File Management
+   - Implement WAV file header parsing and validation
+   - Support multiple sample rates (44.1kHz, 48kHz, 96kHz)
+   - Handle different bit depths (16-bit, 24-bit, 32-bit)
+   - Add sample file discovery and listing capabilities
+   - Acceptance: WAV files properly parsed and validated
+ 
+ - Audio Playback Engine
+   - Implement efficient sample buffering system with double buffering
+   - Add sample rate conversion for mismatched rates
+   - Implement interpolation for smooth playback and pitch shifting
+   - Add loop points and one-shot playback modes
+   - Acceptance: Clean audio playback with no dropouts or glitches
+ 
+ - UART Protocol Integration
+   - Extend existing UART protocol with sample control messages
+   - Add SAMPLE_LOAD, SAMPLE_PLAY, SAMPLE_STOP, SAMPLE_STATUS messages
+   - Implement sample metadata transmission for frontend display
+   - Add sample list and file information requests
+   - Acceptance: Frontend can fully control sample playback via UART
+ 
+ - Audio System Integration
+   - Integrate sample playback with existing audio callback system
+   - Ensure proper mixing with other audio sources (voices, effects)
+   - Maintain stable audio timing and performance
+   - Add performance monitoring and diagnostics
+   - Acceptance: Sample playback integrates seamlessly with audio system
+ 
+ - Testing and Validation Strategy
+   - Stage A: SD card detection and filesystem access
+   - Stage B: WAV file parsing and validation
+   - Stage C: Basic sample playback engine
+   - Stage D: UART protocol integration
+   - Stage E: Full system integration and performance testing
+ 
+ - Implementation Phases
+   - Phase 1: SD card integration and basic file access
+   - Phase 2: WAV parsing and sample buffer management
+   - Phase 3: Playback engine and audio quality optimization
+   - Phase 4: UART integration and system testing
+ 
+ - Non-Goals (for this iteration)
+   - No real-time sample recording (focus on playback first)
+   - No complex sample manipulation beyond basic pitch/speed
+   - No multi-sample layering (single sample playback initially)
  
  ## Rules
  - Follow project coding standards and architecture patterns
  - Ensure all features are fully functional and tested
  - Update documentation and workflow state as work progresses
+ - Maintain audio quality standards for professional applications
+ - Integrate seamlessly with existing UART communication system
  
  ## Items
- - PHASE 1: Prepare repo layout and build flags
- - PHASE 1: Implement SAI1 stereo bring-up and loopback
- - PHASE 1: Implement UART link with framed protocol (migrated from SPI)
- - PHASE 1: Implement MCP4728 CV bus with calibration and 1 kHz flush

+ - PHASE 1: SD Card Hardware Setup and SPI1 Configuration
+ - PHASE 1: SD Card Driver Implementation and FAT32 Access
+ - PHASE 1: Sample File Validation and WAV Header Parsing
+ - PHASE 2: Sample Buffer Management and Memory Optimization
+ - PHASE 2: Playback Engine Core with Timing and Control

  ## Log
```
